title: Cloud Migration Strategy & Tooling
description:
## Migrating Workloads to AWS

Large-scale migrations succeed when they follow a structured program, classify each workload into the right migration strategy, and use the matching AWS tool. SAP-C02 expects you to know the **MAP phases**, the **7 Rs**, and which **migration service** fits each scenario.

### AWS Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) phases

| Phase | What happens |
|---|---|
| **Assess** | Build the business case. Discover the portfolio (Migration Hub / Application Discovery Service), estimate TCO and readiness. |
| **Mobilize** | Create the migration plan and landing zone, close skill gaps, run pilots, refine the cost model and operating model. |
| **Migrate & Modernize** | Execute the migrations wave by wave and modernize workloads (containers, serverless, managed data stores). |

### The 7 Rs of migration

| Strategy | One-line definition |
|---|---|
| **Retire** | Decommission applications that are no longer needed. |
| **Retain** | Keep on-premises (for now) — e.g., compliance, recent investment, or pending decision. |
| **Relocate** | Move infrastructure as-is at the hypervisor level (e.g., VMware Cloud on AWS) with no purchase/rewrite. |
| **Rehost** | "Lift and shift" — move the workload to EC2 with no/minimal code change. |
| **Replatform** | "Lift, tinker, and shift" — small optimizations, e.g., move to Amazon RDS or change the OS, app unchanged. |
| **Repurchase** | Move to a different product, typically SaaS (e.g., self-hosted CRM → Salesforce). |
| **Refactor / Re-architect** | Re-architect the application, often to cloud-native (microservices, serverless) for agility/scale. |

### Key migration tooling

| Tool | Use it for |
|---|---|
| **Application Migration Service (MGN)** | The primary **rehost** tool — continuous **block-level replication** of source servers, then cutover to EC2 with minimal downtime. |
| **Database Migration Service (DMS)** | Migrate/replicate databases with minimal downtime via **change data capture (CDC)**. Pair with the **Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)** for **heterogeneous** migrations (e.g., Oracle → Aurora PostgreSQL). |
| **DataSync** | **Online** bulk and ongoing data transfer over the network (NFS/SMB/S3/EFS/FSx) with verification and scheduling. |
| **Snow Family (Snowball / Snowmobile)** | **Offline** petabyte-scale transfer when bandwidth/time make network transfer impractical. |
| **Migration Hub** | Single pane of glass to **discover, plan, and track** migration progress across tools and accounts. |

### Choosing a data-transfer path

> Compare **volume vs. available bandwidth vs. deadline**. If transferring online would take longer than physically shipping data, choose **Snow**. For continuous or moderate online transfer, choose **DataSync**.

> **Rehost first, modernize later** is a common pattern: get out of the data center quickly with MGN, then refactor high-value workloads in the cloud.

When you're comfortable mapping a workload to a strategy and a tool, mark this complete.
